checks.question: Did the final diff directly resolve FLAG-001 through FLAG-004?
checks.guidance: Verify the shipped code paths, not the plan text or executor notes.

findings.detail: FLAG-001 resolved: `loadTimelineState()` now batch-loads shot names from `shots`, threads `shotNamesById` through `TimelineState`/`ToolContext`, and `viewTimeline()` emits a `Shot groups:` section with resolved shot names or `shotId` fallback.
FLAG-002 resolved: selected-clip shot metadata is preserved across frontend attachments, request payloads, backend normalization, persisted turn attachments, and continuation recovery.
FLAG-003 resolved: `resolveSelectedClipShot()` is the shared backend helper and is now used by `runAgentLoop()`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`.
FLAG-004 resolved: frontend shot lookup now reads `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` in `useSelectedMediaClips()` rather than `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups`.

prior_findings.detail: FLAG-001: timeline summaries could not emit shot names without an explicit shot lookup.
FLAG-002: shot metadata risked being dropped in AgentChat send mapping and turn persistence/recovery.
FLAG-003: same-shot semantics were only partially first-class because several flows still depended on generation lookup directly.
FLAG-004: the plan pointed `useSelectedMediaClips()` at `resolvedConfig.pinnedShotGroups`, which does not exist.

checks.question: Did the implementation honor SD-001 through SD-003?
checks.guidance: Check the concrete call sites for the agreed frontend source, preserved shot-group selection path, and shared shot resolver.

findings.detail: SD-001 confirmed: frontend shot membership resolution uses editor `data.config.pinnedShotGroups` and joins it with `useShots()` for shot names.
SD-002 confirmed: the existing single-click selection path remains `TimelineCanvas -> onSelectClips(group.clipIds) -> TimelineEditor.selectClips`, with no redundant `onShotGroupSelect` path added.
SD-003 confirmed: the shared resolver prefers explicit `shot_id`, then pinned-group membership, then generation fallback, and all required agent flows call it.

checks.question: Do the verification runs substantiate the must criteria?
checks.guidance: Rerun where the executor evidence was narrower than the success criterion.

findings.detail: Re-ran the full `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/` edge suite under `vitest.edge.config.ts`: 7 test files passed, 43 tests passed, 0 failed.
Re-ran the targeted frontend suites covering send-path and shot-group selection behavior: 3 test files passed, 27 tests passed, 0 failed.

pre_check_flags.detail: Diff size looks larger than expected: changed_lines=2056, expected≈10, ratio=205.60, files=44, hunks=142.

criteria.name: `describeTimeline()` output includes a `Shot groups:` section listing each pinned shot group with resolved `shotName` (or `shotId` fallback), `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode` when `pinnedShotGroups` exist.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `viewTimeline()`/`describeTimeline()` now accept `shotNamesById`, render a `Shot groups:` section, and format each group with resolved `shot` label plus `shotId`, `trackId`, `clipIds`, and `mode`; `loadTimelineState()` now loads shot names from `shots`. Covered by `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/timeline.ts`, `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/db.ts`, and `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/timeline.test.ts`.

criteria.name: Each clip line in the timeline summary includes shot metadata when the clip belongs to a pinned shot group.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `formatClipLine()` appends `shot=` and `shotId=` when a clip belongs to a pinned shot group, and `tools/timeline.test.ts` asserts both resolved-name and raw-`shotId` fallback cases.

criteria.name: `SelectedClipPayload` and persisted `AgentTurn.attachments` accept optional shot id/name fields without breaking existing consumers.
criteria.evidence: Passed. Optional `shot_id`/`shot_name` were added to `SelectedClipPayload`, and optional `shotId`/`shotName` were added to persisted `AgentTurnAttachment`; the additions are additive-only. `selectedClips.test.ts`, `useAgentSession.test.tsx`, and `loop.test.ts` cover the round-trip behavior.

criteria.name: The frontend selection send path (`useSelectedMediaClips` -> `AgentChat` -> `useSendMessage`) preserves `shot_id` and `shot_name` for timeline-selected clips.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `useSelectedMediaClips()` resolves shot metadata from `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups` plus `useShots()`, `AgentChat` preserves `shotId`/`shotName` when merging timeline and gallery selections, and `useSendMessage()` serializes `shot_id`/`shot_name`. Verified in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts`, `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx`, `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts`, `AgentChat.test.tsx`, and `useAgentSession.test.tsx`.

criteria.name: Backend selected-clip normalization and continuation recovery preserve shot metadata across turns.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `normalizeSelectedClips()` trims and retains `shot_id`/`shot_name`, `attachSelectedClips()` persists them onto user-turn attachments, and `recoverSelectedClipsFromTurns()` restores them on continuation turns. Covered by `selectedClips.ts`, `loop.ts`, `selectedClips.test.ts`, and `loop.test.ts`.

criteria.name: `buildSelectedClipsPrompt()` includes shot info when `shot_id` or `shot_name` is available.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `buildSelectedClipsPrompt()` now emits `shot_id=` and `shot_name="..."` alongside generation and timeline context. `loop.test.ts` explicitly asserts both fields appear in the prompt.

criteria.name: Same-shot resolution is centralized and used by `runAgentLoop`, `executeCreateTask()`, `executeSetLora()`, and `executeDuplicateGeneration()`, preferring explicit shot metadata and falling back to generation lookup only when needed.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `resolveSelectedClipShot()` implements explicit-shot, pinned-group, then generation-fallback precedence, and the four required call sites now use it. Verified in `tools/clips.ts`, `loop.ts`, `tools/create-task.ts`, `tools/loras.ts`, `tools/duplicate-generation.ts`, plus `tools/clips.test.ts` and `tools/create-task.test.ts`.

criteria.name: `executeDuplicateGeneration()` skips `findShotForGenerations()` when selected clips already provide a shared `shot_id`.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `executeDuplicateGeneration()` now delegates to `resolveSelectedClipShot()` with `additionalGenerationIds`; the explicit-shot fast path returns before any generation-history lookup. `tools/clips.test.ts` asserts the database `.in()` lookup is not called when selected clips already share `shot_id`.

criteria.name: Clicking a shot-group label continues to select all clips in that group.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `TimelineCanvas` still calls `onSelectClips?.(group.clipIds)` on single-click, `TimelineEditor` still wires `onSelectClips={selectClips}`, and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` asserts a single click selects `['clip-1','clip-2','clip-3']`.

criteria.name: All existing tests in `supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/` continue to pass.
criteria.evidence: Passed. Re-ran the full edge suite: `command-parser.test.ts`, `selectedClips.test.ts`, `loop.test.ts`, `tool-calls.test.ts`, `tools/timeline.test.ts`, `tools/create-task.test.ts`, and `tools/clips.test.ts` all passed. Totals: 7 files, 43 tests, 0 failures.

criteria.name: New or updated unit tests cover timeline shot-group summaries, selected-clip shot metadata roundtrip, selected-clip prompt shot context, duplicate-generation's direct `shot_id` path, and the frontend send path.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `tools/timeline.test.ts` covers shot-group summaries and clip-level shot context; `selectedClips.test.ts` and `loop.test.ts` cover shot metadata round-trip and prompt/system-prompt shot context; `tools/clips.test.ts` covers resolver precedence and duplicate-generation direct `shot_id` fast path; `AgentChat.test.tsx` and `useAgentSession.test.tsx` cover frontend merge/send preservation.

criteria.name: The system prompt mentions shared-shot context guidance once structured shot metadata is available.
criteria.evidence: Passed. `buildTimelineAgentSystemPrompt()` now emits a shared-shot guidance line with `shot_id`, optional `shot_name`, and reuse guidance when a shared shot is known; `loop.test.ts` asserts that guidance appears.

criteria.name: No new files exceed roughly 300 lines.
criteria.evidence: Passed in scope. The claimed implementation updated existing files only; `git diff --name-status` across the scoped task files shows only `M` entries and no added scoped files. The dirty worktree contains unrelated untracked files, but they are outside the claimed change set.

criteria.name: Manual smoke test: select a shot group, send an agent message, and verify shot context survives into a continuation turn.
criteria.evidence: Waived in automated review. I did not perform the manual UI smoke flow here. The executor's temporary edge-Vitest repro gives additional confidence, but it is not a manual smoke test.

summary: Approved. All must criteria pass. I re-verified FLAG-001 through FLAG-004 directly against the code, confirmed the settled decisions were implemented correctly, reran the full `ai-timeline-agent` edge suite (7 files, 43 tests), and reran the targeted frontend suites (3 files, 27 tests). The worktree is noisy, but the scoped implementation under review satisfies the success criteria.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Current `TimelineCanvas`/`TimelineEditor` code preserves the original shot-group single-click selection contract and does not add a redundant selection callback.
Pass. The shared selected-clip boundary was extended additively with optional shot metadata on both frontend and backend types.
Pass. Frontend shot resolution uses `data?.config.pinnedShotGroups`, merged attachments retain shot metadata, and the send payload includes `shot_id`/`shot_name`.
Pass. Backend normalization, attachment persistence, and continuation recovery now round-trip shot metadata without overwriting explicit client values.
Pass. Timeline state now loads shot names and both loop-time summaries and `run(command="view")` share the same shot-aware timeline formatter.
Pass. Same-shot semantics are centralized in `resolveSelectedClipShot()` and used by loop defaults, task creation, LoRA updates, and duplicate-generation.

Pass. Coverage was added by extending existing suites in place, including timeline summaries, round-trip metadata, prompt/system-prompt shot context, duplicate fast path, and frontend send-path preservation.
Pass. Verification is complete for the automated scope: I re-ran the full edge suite and the targeted frontend suites successfully. The manual smoke criterion remains informational and waived.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed by current TimelineCanvas/TimelineEditor code and the passing TimelineCanvas suite.
Confirmed by additive type changes in `agent-session.ts` and `types.ts`, plus passing serialization/send tests.
Confirmed by `useSelectedMediaClips()`, `AgentChat`, and `useSendMessage()` code paths and passing frontend tests.
Confirmed by `selectedClips.ts` and `loop.ts` round-trip logic and passing edge tests.
Confirmed by `db.ts`, `registry.ts`, and `timeline.ts`, plus timeline summary test coverage.
Confirmed by the shared resolver implementation and its use in loop, create-task, set-lora, and duplicate-generation paths.
Confirmed by updated existing suites only; no new scoped test files were needed.
Confirmed for the automated scope by rerunning the full edge suite and targeted frontend suites successfully.
